config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama; kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan dasar seseorang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, ketika “kepentingan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, sering kali yang kuat jadi lebih mudah menekan yang lemah. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati, lalu kepentingan umum diatur dengan adil dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank individual rights, because that logic can be abused very easily. From my U.S. perspective, the whole point of rights is to put a hard limit on what the majority, the government, or a loud public can demand from one person. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case; public safety, disease control, and basic order can justify some limits, but only narrowly and with real accountability. If “society first” becomes the default, it often ends up meaning the powerful get to decide whose rights matter.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan pada kelompok kecil, padahal yang seharusnya dilindungi justru orang yang paling rentan. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa paling berhak. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat dan kebebasan dasar orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that starts with the good of the many, because in practice it can become a blank check for government, majorities, or institutions to trample unpopular people. At the same time, I do not buy the idea that individual rights exist in a vacuum, since real public needs like safety, public health, and fair rules sometimes require limits. The key is that those limits have to be narrow, justified, and accountable, or “society first” becomes just a polite way to excuse abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, minoritas, atau mereka yang tidak punya kuasa membela diri. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau kesehatan bersama yang boleh membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” hanya jadi alasan sopan untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be allowed to override individual rights as a default rule. From my U.S. perspective, I have seen how quickly “for the common good” can turn into a convenient excuse for censorship, discrimination, or government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. Public safety and health can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are specific, necessary, and accountable. If society always comes first, then individual rights stop being rights and become privileges the powerful can take away whenever it suits them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
